papers.title: Leakage Power Minimization Techniques In CMOS Inverter Circuits
papers.venue: Journal of Karary University for Engineering and Science
papers.abstract: Power efficiency has become a paramount design constraint in modern CMOS integrated circuits, particularly as technology scaling leads to significant static power dissipation due to leakage currents. This work presents a comprehensive evaluation and implementation of three prominent leakage-reduction techniques: Forced Transistor Stacking (FTS), Leakage Control Transistor (LECTOR), and Leakage-Control P/N MOS (LC P/N MOS). These techniques are implemented for a CMOS inverter circuit using DSCH and Microwind 2.6 design tools in a 120nm CMOS process. The methods are systematically compared in terms of leakage power suppression, propagation delay, and area overhead. Simulation results demonstrate that the LC P/N MOS technique achieves a remarkable 99.98% reduction in static power dissipation compared to a standard inverter, albeit with a slight increase in delay. The LECTOR technique offers the best performance with a mere 5ps delay, while the Stack technique provides a balanced trade-off. The findings provide clear guidelines for selecting Leakage reduction strategies based on application-specific requirements of power, performance, and area.

papers.title: Field-Assisted Photon Emission in Single-Electron Quantum Dot Structures
papers.venue: Global Power, Energy and Communication Conference
papers.abstract: Quantum dots (QD) have recently attracted attention for their electrical, optical and mechanical properties. When used in electronic circuits, their electrical and optical properties are affected by Coulomb blockade while the flow of electrons is achieved by single electron tunneling events. This paper studies the optical properties of the dots under the influence of electric fields. The scenarios presented include single and double quantum dot structures. It is shown that the patterns of photon emission are strongly dependent on the electric field and the number of dots together with the details of the discrete energy structure of the dots.

papers.title: Improving Leakage Power Reduction in CMOS Inverter Circuits Using LECTOR Techniques
papers.venue: Journal of Karary University for Engineering and Science
papers.abstract: With the rapid growth of the semiconductor industry and the increasing complexity of applications using small-sized chips, CMOS technology has become the standard for integrated circuit design. However, higher circuit speeds lead to increased power dissipation, primarily due to leakage currents. This paper presents the LECTOR (Leakage Control Transistor) technique, which utilizes two additional transistors to mitigate leakage currents in CMOS circuits. Simulations using Microwind and DSCH software at 250nm technology demonstrated an average leakage power reduction of 7.5%. The proposed technique effectively reduces static power without compromising dynamic performance

papers.title: Deployment of RIS in MIMO OFDM Dual-Function Radar-Communication System
papers.abstract: Dual-function radar-communication (DFRC) system is considered one of the most promising technologies in wireless communication systems. By using orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM), the system can sense several spatial directions while serving multiple users concurrently. Multi-input multi-output (MIMO) DFRC with the aid of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is examined in this paper. Using genetic algorithms, this paper optimizes the mutual information (MI) performance metric for the targets while ensuring a minimum error rate for the users. For the scenarios considered in this paper, it is shown that the proper RIS deployment can improve the system performance by 20%.

papers.title: Multi-RIS-Aided Secure MIMO Dual-Function Radar-Communication System Based on Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio
papers.venue: IEEE Advanced Information Technology, Electronic and Automation Control Conference
papers.abstract: In this paper, we introduce multiple Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RISs) aided secure MIMO Dual-Function Radar-Communication (DFRC) system for enhanced dual-functionality performance. We aim to optimize the Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) as a performance metric for radar, while simultaneously meeting the secrecy rate requirements for the communication link. Major effort is given to optimize multiple RISs configurations that counteract the presence of eavesdroppers and actively manage the propagation environment to benefit legitimate user. The optimization problem is solved iteratively by using CVX and the majorization-minimization (MM) algorithm. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed method with multiple RISs can improve SINR for radar target detection and also secure communication link, which in turn enhances system performance. The proposed model is used to determine the optimum directions for RISs deployment.

papers.title: A Damping-Free Method for Mitigation of Trapezoidal Rule Oscillations in Linear Systems
papers.venue: Global Power, Energy and Communication Conference
papers.abstract: This paper presents a novel method for mitigating trapezoidal solver oscillations (trapezoidal ringing) by averaging them in real-time and without damping. The developed method represents a first-of-its-kind approach where efforts hitherto have focused on damping. Method derivation and conceptual development is given and illustrated with a simple circuit example. A diode benchmarking circuit is used for testing the developed method in comparison to typical damping methods with several damping factors against smaller solution time steps. Testing included an exact solution implementation of the developed method and an approximately exact implementation reserved for real-time applications. The results show superior performance compared to the typical damping methods where damping by its nature introduces amplitude and phase distortion. The method is applicable to linear or piecewise-linear systems and is hence applicable to a large variety of electromagnetic transient (EMT) problem cases.

papers.title: Conductance Oscillations in Asymmetric Quantum Dot Structures
papers.abstract: The electrical transfer characteristics of asymmetric quantum dot structures are studied using a master equation model. The patterns of oscillations caused by Coulomb blockade and charge trapping at the quantum dots are investigated in relation to the capacitors coupling the dots to the gate electrode. The model is used to study double and triple quantum dot structures and the dynamics of charge transfer are discussed. It is shown that the capacitor mismatch results in different transconductance patterns, and the period of oscillations is found to depend on the smallest dot-gate coupling capacitance and the relative values of capacitances.

papers.title: Study of single-electron tunneling oscillations using monte-carlo based modeling algorithms to a capacitive slanted two-dimensional array of tunnel junctions
Model Predictive-Actor Critic Reinforcement Learning for Dexterous Manipulation
papers.venue: 2020 International Conference on Computer, Control, Electrical, and Electronics Engineering (ICCCEEE)
papers.abstract: Dexterous multi-fingered robotic hands represent a promising solution for robotic manipulators to perform a wide range of complex tasks, through acquiring more general purpose skills. Nevertheless, developing complex behaviours for a robot needs sophisticated control strategies, which requires domain expertise with a good understanding of mathematics, and underlying physics, this will be very difficult for such complex robots. Learning algorithms like deep reinforcement learning provide a general framework for robots to learn complex behaviours directly from data. But, even for learning algorithms, dexterous manipulators represent a major challenge due to their high dimensionality and contact rich under-actuated object manipulation. To overcome these challenges, learning algorithms demand extensive data generation, which is often expensive or hard to obtain. So, the need for more efficient and more effective algorithms -that extract more useful information from the least amount of available data- has increased. This paper presents a contribution which is the development of the algorithm Model Predictive Control-Soft Actor Critic (MPC-SAC), an algorithm that combines offline learning with online planning to develop a control policy. The algorithm is benchmarked on two challenging complex dexterous manipulation tasks, against other state of the art model free reinforcement learning algorithms. Finally, it was found that the algorithm achieves asymptotic performance with state of the art data efficiency in both tasks.

papers.title: Sesame Seed Disease Detection Using Image Classification
papers.venue: 2020 International Conference on Computer, Control, Electrical, and Electronics Engineering (ICCCEEE)
papers.abstract: Plant diseases cause significant losses in production, economy, quality, and quantity of agricultural products. The economy of Sudan is highly dependent on agriculture. The international demand for Sudanese sesame seeds was remarkably stable throughout 2018. However, the agriculture sector’s contribution to GDP (Gross Domestic Product) growth rate has decreased from 4% in 2018 to 1.2% in 2019. One of the main contributors to the decrease in the contribution of the GDP was diseases. The early detection of diseases is vital in agriculture for efficient crop yield. One of the leading technologies used worldwide for disease detection is machine learning and Specifically Convolutional Neural Networks, which classify images of diseased plants/leaves from healthy plants/leaves. This research compares a developed CNN model and five other ready-made models; VGG16, VGG19, Resnet50, Resnet101, and Resnet152. The dataset contains 1,695 images of sesame leaves grouped into three classes, two of which are of diseases currently affecting the Sesame in Sudan, and the third is of the healthy leaves. The leaves are photographed from different fields in Gadarif State. The developed model achieved the best result with a training accuracy of 90.77% and testing accuracy of 88.5%. Future work and possible improvements to this model were also discussed.

papers.title: Coherence of oscillations generated by single-electronic two-dimensional arrays of tunnel junctions
papers.abstract: The single-electron devices (SEDs) are novel devices based on a new operation concept and technologies, with great scaling potential that has been fabricated and is under investigation. The SEDs are based on the controllable transfer of single electrons (SEs) between small conducting electrodes. They have had already several demonstrative scientific experiments as well as enabled fabrication methods. The present work studies the coherence of signals in a two-dimensional homogeneous arrays of small tunnel junctions. The Monte-Carlo technique is used to simulate the circuit and collect the required statistics. The simulator is a hybrid simulator that uses at the beginning the Master Equation representation to compute the steady-state current through the circuit. It is shown, how the coherence of oscillations is strengthened with longer arrays structures, and that the coupled two-dimensional long arrays are having strong correlated tunnel events, when the finite frequency noise characteristics and oscillations pattern variation are addressed. The variation of both the applied voltage and the coupling capacitors is illustrated. The dependency of the transport process and distribution of time between the events on their setting parameters is shown and the coefficient of variation was calculated to clarify the results. Also, the state’s transition flow is reviewed for the traverse of electrons through the arrays structure for different bias conditions.
